app.description: Multi-tenant AI orchestration platform with hot/cold tier pooling, BYOK, and support for multiple frameworks.

profile.title: Profile
profile.apiKeysCreateDescription: Give the key a label, then pick a role tab. The key receives that role’s permissions as OAuth scopes.
profile.apiKeysRolesDescription: Each tab is a role defined in Admin → Global settings. The API key is created with that role’s permission set.
profile.apiKeysNoRoles: No roles are defined yet. Create roles under Global settings → Custom roles.
profile.apiKeysRoleNoPermissions: This role has no permissions — choose another role or add permissions to the role first.
profile.apiKeysRoleScopesHint: These strings are sent as API key scopes; the server must accept each permission.
profile.apiKeysPresetHint: Default: full platform admin permission for automation.
profile.apiKeysAdvancedScopes: Advanced: enter scopes manually (one per line)
profile.apiKeysHeaderXApiKeyDesc: User API key (cdk_…). Use for scripts and tools.
profile.apiKeysHeaderBearerDesc: OAuth2 access token (JWT). Use after browser or token endpoint login.
profile.apiKeysCodeExamples: Example: GET /api/me with an API key
profile.apiKeysCodeExamplesHint: Replace YOUR_API_KEY with the secret shown once when you create a key. You can use this app’s origin or your Cadence API base URL; the UI proxy forwards X-API-KEY to the API.
profile.apiKeysExpiresAtOptionalHint: Leave empty for no expiry. After this time the key stops working.
profile.apiKeysRevokeConfirmMessage: This key will stop working immediately. This cannot be undone.
profile.apiKeysScopesRequired: Pick a role that has at least one permission.
profile.apiKeysRawKeyWarning: Copy it now — you will not be able to see the full key again.
profile.apiKeysRawKeyDescription: Store it in a password manager or secret store. Anyone with this key can act as your account within the granted scopes.

settings.saveAiAppDefaultsMessage: These values will pre-fill new AI App instances. Existing instances are not affected.

admin.rateChatLimitRpmHint: Requests per minute for chat completion endpoint

admin.platformModelCatalogDesc: Manage the global list of available models for each provider. Disabled models will not appear in provider model selections.
admin.disableModelConfirm: Disable this model in the catalog?
admin.enableModelConfirm: Enable this model in the catalog?
admin.modelCategoryHint: Text generation is fully supported; image, TTS, and STT are placeholders for future billing.

langGraphSupervisor.defaultLlmConfigDescription: LLM configuration used by all nodes unless overridden per node.
langGraphSupervisor.defaultModelDescription: Model used by all nodes unless overridden per node.
langGraphSupervisor.placeholderNoConfigs: No LLM configurations available — add one in Settings first.
langGraphSupervisor.defaultMaxTokensDescription: Default max output tokens for all nodes (Min/Max: 1k/4k)
langGraphSupervisor.nodeExecutionTimeoutDescription: Default timeout in seconds for each graph node or agent step (LLM and tool calls; answer nodes use 2×).
langGraphSupervisor.maxMessageContextWindowDescription: Last N messages sent to the AI App (Min/Max: 5/50).
langGraphSupervisor.autocompactLlmConfigDescription: LLM configuration used for message compaction.
langGraphSupervisor.suggestionLlmConfigDescription: LLM configuration used to generate suggested questions.
langGraphSupervisor.suggestionModelDescription: Model used for generating suggestion questions

langGraphSupervisor.modelNodeConfigDescription: Model for this node's LLM configuration.
langGraphSupervisor.modelOverrideDescription: Overrides the default model for this node.
langGraphSupervisor.maxTokensDescription: Overrides this node's maximum output tokens.
langGraphSupervisor.timeoutDescription: Overrides the timeout for this node in seconds.
langGraphSupervisor.promptOverrideDescription: Must contain the same {placeholders} as the default prompt.
langGraphSupervisor.placeholderEmptyPrompt: Leave empty to use the default prompt

langGraphSupervisor.promptOverridePlaceholderBuiltin: Uses the built-in default prompt when empty.

langGraphSupervisor.modelNameChooseCatalogHint: Current value is not in the catalog; change the text to match a listed model id or pick from the list after switching

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.description:
**What it does:**

Central hub for your organization's AI orchestration. Shows:

- **AI App counts** — Total, hot and cold tier instances
- **Pool statistics** — Shared models, memory usage (sys admin only)
- **Quick actions** — Start chat, view AI Apps, manage AI agents

**Tiers:** Hot = loaded immediately; cold = on-demand (demand pool may retain recently used instances).

chat.title: Chat
chat.description:
**What it does:**

Interactive playground for testing AI Apps with real conversations.

**How to use:**
1. Select an AI App from the sidebar (it must be active and ready)
2. Type a message and press Enter
3. View the response, agent steps, and tool sources

**Features:**
- Streaming responses with progress
- Expandable steps and sources for each message
- Session persists until you clear it

agentStore.title: Agents Store
agentStore.description:
**What it does:**

Browse and manage AI agents (plugin ZIPs) that extend AI App capabilities.

**Types:**
- **Official** — Platform-wide, available to all organizations
- **Org** — Per-organization, uploaded by org admins

**Actions:** Enable/disable, view details, configure settings. Each package maps to the plugin API (`/api/.../plugins`) and provides the tools AI Apps use at runtime.

profile.title: Profile
profile.description:
**What it does:**

Your personal account settings.

**Sections:**
- **Account** — User ID, role (read-only)
- **Profile** — Display name, email, avatar URL, locale, timezone, bio
- **API keys** (system admins) — Create and revoke keys

**Password:** Use **Change password** in the user menu (⋯). Changes apply immediately.

aiApps.title: AI Apps
aiApps.description:
**What it does:**

Create and manage AI App instances that run your AI agents (plugin packages).

**Actions:**
- **Load** — Start the AI App (uses pool memory)
- **Unload** — Stop and free memory
- **Activate** — Enable for use; **Deactivate** — Disable
- **Clone** — Copy config to create a new instance

**Columns:** Name, framework, mode, tier, status, ready indicator.

orgIdentity.title: Organization
orgIdentity.description:
**What it shows:**

Read-only identity details for your current organization:

- **Organization ID** — Unique identifier
- **Slug** — URL-friendly name
- **Domain** — Assigned domain (for example, acme.com)
- **Subscription tier** — free, pro, enterprise
- **Status** — active, suspended, etc.

**Note:** Only system admins can change these values.

orgProfile.title: Organization Profile
orgProfile.description:
**What it does:**

Editable public profile visible to org members.

**Fields:**
- Display name, contact email, website
- Logo URL, country, timezone
- Description (brief org summary)

**Who can edit:** Org admins only. Used for branding and contact info.

members.title: Members
members.description:
**What it does:**

Manage who belongs to your organization.

**Actions:**
- **Add member** — By email, username, or user ID
- **Promote** — Grant org admin rights
- **Demote** — Remove org admin rights
- **Remove** — Remove from the organization

**Who can manage:** Org admins only. Members can belong to multiple organizations.

llmConfigs.title: LLM Configurations
llmConfigs.description:
**What it does:**

Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) — store API keys for LLM providers.

**Supported:** OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Groq, Azure, LiteLLM, TensorZero, Bifrost

**Per config:** Name, provider, API key, optional base URL, API version (Azure)

**Usage:** AI Apps select a config when running. Disabled configs are hidden. Configs in use cannot be deleted until unlinked.

centralPoints.title: Central Points
centralPoints.description:
**What it does:**

Stable API endpoints that abstract AI App identity.

**Why use them:**
- Clients use a **central point ID** — never the AI App ID
- Swap AI Apps behind the endpoint without client changes
- Private (auth required) or public (no auth)

**Flow:** Create central point → assign AI App → clients call `/api/chat/completion` with `X-CENTRAL-ID` header.

aiApps.title: AI App Settings
aiApps.description:
**What it does:**

Default values pre-filled when creating **new** AI Apps.

**Defaults:**
- Default LLM config
- Default model name
- Default max tokens
- Node execution timeout (seconds)

**Important:** Existing AI Apps are **not** affected. Only new instances get these values.

orgs.title: Organizations
orgs.description:
**What it does:**

System-wide organization management (sys admin only).

**Actions:**
- Create organizations (name, domain, tier)
- View and edit org details
- Set tier quotas and rate limits

**Tier quotas** control max AI Apps, members, LLM configs, messages, etc. per organization.

orgDetail.title: Organization Details
orgDetail.description:
**What it does:**

Edit a single organization (system admins only).

**Sections:**
- **Profile** — Display name, domain, tier, contact info
- **Tier quotas** — Max AI Apps, members, central points, messages, LLM configs, rate limits
- **Members** — Add, promote, demote, remove

**Preview** shows current usage versus quota limits.

users.title: Users
users.description:
**What it does:**

Platform user management (sys admin only).

**Actions:**
- Create users (username, email, optional password)
- Edit username, email, system admin flag
- Delete users (removes all org memberships)

**System admin** — Full platform access. Users can belong to multiple organizations with different roles.

systemPlugins.title: Agent Store
systemPlugins.description:
**What it does:**

Manage AI agents (plugin ZIPs) available to **all** organizations (system admins only).

**Tabs:** Official | Org (per organization)

**Actions:**
- Upload AI agent (ZIP)
- Enable/disable versions
- View details and configuration

Org admins enable official agents for their organization. Organization-scoped agents are uploaded per organization in **Agent Store**.

globalSettings.title: Global Settings
globalSettings.description:
**What it does:**

Platform-wide defaults (sys admin only).

**Sections:**
- **Subscription tiers** — Quota limits per tier (AI Apps, members, messages, etc.)
- **LLM providers** — Model catalog per provider
- **Feature settings** — Pool, caching, rate limiting, etc.

**Override:** Some settings can be overridden per-organization. Tier quotas are global.

pool.title: Instance Pool
pool.description:
**What it does:**

Real-time pool statistics (sys admin only).

**Metrics:**
- Total instances, hot/cold tier counts
- Shared models and bundles
- Memory estimate

**Auto-refresh:** Every 30 seconds. Use Refresh to update manually.

health.title: System Health
health.description:
**What it does:**

System and service status (sys admin only).

**Checks:**
- **PostgreSQL, Redis, MongoDB** — Database connectivity
- **AI App instances** — Loaded instances, ready state

**Use for:** Diagnosing outages, verifying instance health before traffic.

displayName.title: Display Name
displayName.description:
**What it is:**

The public name shown in the UI and integrations.

**Use for:** Branding, user identification, and contact display. It is not used for authentication.

provider.title: Provider
provider.description:
**What it is:**

The LLM provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc.).

**Effect:** Determines the API format and endpoint. Each provider has different models and pricing. Select the provider that owns your API key.

model.title: Model
model.description:
**What it is:**

The model identifier (for example, `gpt-4o`, `gemini-2.0-flash`).

**Rule:** It must be supported by the selected provider. Check the provider documentation for valid model IDs. Use the catalog for platform-managed models.

apiKey.title: API Key
apiKey.description:
**What it is:**

Your API key for the provider.

**Security:** Stored securely in encrypted form. Never shown in logs or the UI after saving. Rotate keys if compromised.

endpoint.title: Endpoint URL
endpoint.description:
**What it is:**

Stable URL for chat completion: `POST /api/chat/completion`

**Headers required:**
- `X-ORG-ID` — Organization ID
- `X-CENTRAL-ID` — Central point ID
- `Authorization: Bearer` — For private (optional for public)

apiKey.title: API Key
apiKey.description:
**Private central points:**

Clients must send `Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN` in every request.

**Public:** No auth header required. Use for open demos or internal tools.

framework.title: Framework
framework.description:
**What it is:**

The orchestration framework:

- **LangGraph** — Multi-node graphs and state machines
- **Google ADK** — Agent Development Kit
- **OpenAI Agents** — OpenAI agent framework

**Effect:** Determines the graph structure, node types, and execution model. Immutable after creation.

llmConfig.title: LLM Configuration
llmConfig.description:
**What it is:**

LLM config used by this AI App for inference.

**Source:** Must be from your org's LLM Configurations (Settings → LLM Configs).

**Supervisor mode:** Can override per-node. Non-supervisor uses this config globally.

createBasic.title: Basic
createBasic.description:
**What it does:**

Core identity and runtime settings for the new AI App.

**Fields:**
- **Name** — Unique identifier (10–55 chars). Used in URLs and API.
- **Framework** — LangGraph, Google ADK, or OpenAI Agents. **Immutable after creation.**
- **Mode** — Supervisor (multi-agent) or other modes. Depends on framework.
- **Initial tier** — Hot (load immediately) or cold (on-demand). Affects pool memory.

createPlugins.title: AI agents
createPlugins.description:
**What it does:**

Select AI agents (plugin packages) that add capabilities to this AI App.

**Flow:**
1. Click **Add AI agent** to open the selector
2. Search for and add one or more agents (required)
3. Configure each agent's settings below the table

**Note:** Each agent can have required fields. Validation runs before creation. You can remove agents before saving.

supervisorLlmConfig.title: Supervisor Node Settings
supervisorLlmConfig.description:
**What it does:**

Override LLM settings for each node in the supervisor graph.

**Nodes:**
- **Router** — Classifies incoming messages
- **Planner** — Plans steps
- **Synthesizer** — Produces the final response
- **Clarifier, Responder, Validation, Error Handler** — Specialized nodes

**Per node:** Override the LLM config, model, temperature, max tokens, and timeout. Leave empty to use the default from the LLM Config card.

nodeConfig.title: Node Config
nodeConfig.description:
**What it does:**

Override LLM settings per node in the supervisor graph.

**Nodes:**
- **Router** — Classifies incoming messages
- **Planner** — Plans steps
- **Synthesizer** — Final response
- **Clarifier, Responder, Validation, Error Handler** — Specialized nodes

**Per node:** Override LLM config, model, temperature, max tokens, timeout. Leave empty to use the default from LLM Config above.

editBasic.title: Basic
editBasic.description:
**What it does:**

Edit metadata and tier. Framework and mode are **immutable** after creation.

**Editable:**
- **Name** — Display name (10–200 chars)
- **Tier** — Hot or cold

**Supervisor only:** LLM config is in the Supervisor Settings section below.

**Non-supervisor:** Select LLM config here — it's used for all inference.

additionalSettings.title: Additional Settings
additionalSettings.description:
**What it does:**

This card sets context windows and timeouts for this AI App.

**Fields:**
- **Message context window** — Max messages kept in context per turn.
- **Max context window (tokens)** — Token budget for context.
- **Node execution timeout** — Timeout in seconds per node.
- **Max agent hops / tool rounds** — Loop safety limits (where shown).

**Monitoring, follow-up suggestions, and auto-compact** live in the row below. Each card’s **header switch** is the feature on/off control: on expands the card and enables that feature; off folds the card and disables it (no separate enable control inside the card).

llmConfig.title: LLM Config
llmConfig.description:
**What it does:**

Sets the default LLM used by all nodes in this AI App.

**Fields:**
- **Default LLM config** — Required. Select the provider + credential config from your org.
- **Default model** — Model used by all nodes unless overridden per-node.
- **Temperature** — Controls output randomness (0.0–2.0).
- **Max tokens** — Max output tokens per response (Min/Max: 1k/4k).

**Note:** These defaults can be overridden per-node in the Supervisor Node Settings card.

centralPoints.selectOrgDescription: Choose an organization from the menu above to manage central points.
centralPoints.title: Central Points
centralPoints.description: Stable API endpoints for AI Apps. Clients use a central point ID, so you can swap AI Apps without changing the client.
centralPoints.noCenterPoints: No central points yet. Create one to get started.
centralPoints.name: Name
centralPoints.editSubtitle: Edit settings and view API integration guides.
centralPoints.apiIntegrationDescription: Use the central point ID below to send chat requests. The endpoint stays stable, so you can swap AI Apps at any time without updating your clients.
centralPoints.privateAuthDescription: This central point requires authentication. Include an `Authorization: Bearer` token header in all requests.
centralPoints.publicNoAuthDescription: This central point is publicly accessible. No authentication header is required.

aiApps.loadConfirm: Load AI App "{name}"? This will start the AI App.
aiApps.unloadConfirm: Unload AI App "{name}"? This will stop the AI App.

create.title: Create AI App
create.addPluginHint: Add at least one AI agent to configure.
create.whoamiPlaceholder: Optional context about who this AI App is (e.g. role, domain, tone). Injected into answer nodes.
create.nameMinLength: Name must be at least 10 characters
create.selectPluginRequired: Select at least one AI agent
create.llmConfigRequired: Default LLM Config is required for Supervisor mode
create.pluginsEmptyDesc: Add AI agents to extend your AI App's capabilities
create.pluginConfigurationDesc: Configure settings for each active AI agent.

supervisor.maxAgentHopsHelp: Maximum node switches per graph run (loop safety).
supervisor.maxToolRoundsHelp: Maximum completed executor rounds (planner → executor) per invocation.

grounded.predefinedContextDesc: Static context injected into every turn. Use it for general instructions.
grounded.scopeRulesDesc: Define what is in scope and out of scope. Leave empty to auto-generate from the anchor record.
grounded.scopeRulesPlaceholder: Leave empty to auto-generate from the record…
grounded.maxAgentHopsDesc: Maximum planner node visits per graph run (default: 25).
grounded.maxToolRoundsDesc: Maximum completed tool batches per user message (default: 3). When the limit is reached, pending tool calls are closed and the answer is synthesized. Validator replays do not count toward this limit.
grounded.enabledValidatorDesc: Adds a validation step to check that responses stay within the defined scope.
grounded.singlePluginHint: Grounded mode requires exactly one scoped AI agent.

pluginSelector.title: Add AI agent
pluginSelector.noPluginsMatch: No AI agents match the filter.

edit.title: Edit AI App
edit.nameMinLength: Name must be at least 10 characters
edit.nameMaxLength: Name must be at most 200 characters
edit.llmConfigForOrchestrator: LLM config for this AI App
edit.monitoringDescription: Send traces to an observability provider
edit.langfuseFieldsRequired: Secret Key, Public Key, and Host URL are required when monitoring is enabled
